title: Gobi Paratha

subs.name: a squeeze of fresh lemon into the filling

steps.text: Knead atta with salt and water into a soft, smooth dough. Cover and rest 20 minutes.

Grate the cauliflower on the fine side of a box grater. Add 0.5 tsp salt, toss, and leave for 5 minutes. Then squeeze out as much moisture as possible using your hands or a clean cloth. This step is essential — wet filling tears the paratha.
Combine the squeezed cauliflower with green chilli, ginger, coriander leaves, cumin seeds, red chilli powder, amchur and salt. Taste and adjust — the filling should be flavourful on its own.

Divide the dough into 8 balls. Roll one into a 10 cm disc, place 2 tbsp of filling in the centre, and pinch the edges shut. Flatten and roll gently to an 18 cm round, keeping the filling even throughout.

Cook on a medium-hot tawa for about 90 seconds on the first side until dry specks appear. Flip, add a drizzle of ghee, and cook another minute. Flip once more, add more ghee if needed, and press with a spatula until both sides are golden and crisp.
Serve at once with curd, butter and your favourite pickle. Gobi parathas soften quickly, so bring them straight from the tawa to the table.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: cauliflower → finely grated radish (mooli), squeezed dry (classic mooli paratha variation); amchur → a squeeze of fresh lemon into the filling; ghee → butter or oil
